audienceSections.normal: In a description of Layla’s room, you will use [[grammar:فِي + place]]. Put [[grammar:فِي]] before a place to mean ‘in.’ In فِي الْغُرْفَةِ مَكْتَبٌ, الْغُرْفَةِ is the place and مَكْتَبٌ is what is there. Do not put فِي after the place.
audienceSections.expert: This chapter presents [[grammar:فِي + place]] in a description of Layla’s room. The preposition فِي heads a locative prepositional phrase and assigns genitive case to الْغُرْفَةِ. The postposed indefinite مَكْتَبٌ is the nominative theme of the verbless existential clause.

audienceSections.normal: Put [[grammar:فِي]] before a place to mean ‘in.’ In فِي الْغُرْفَةِ مَكْتَبٌ, الْغُرْفَةِ is the place and مَكْتَبٌ is what is there. Do not put فِي after the place.
audienceSections.expert: The preposition فِي heads a locative prepositional phrase and assigns genitive case to الْغُرْفَةِ. The postposed indefinite مَكْتَبٌ is the nominative theme of the verbless existential clause.

audienceSections.normal: The Arabic lines are in Modern Standard Arabic. The vowel marks show short vowels and case endings, so read every marked vowel instead of guessing from the bare consonants.
audienceSections.expert: The reading uses fully vocalized Modern Standard Arabic. Lexical citation forms follow the established perfect-stem convention for verbs, while the learner text preserves the attested inflected form and case vocalization.

breakdown.normal:
Read each exact arabic line, then check its natural English meaning. Watch for [[grammar:فِي + place]] where it appears.

- هَذِهِ غُرْفَةُ لَيْلَى. — This is Layla's room.
- الْغُرْفَةُ فِي الْبَيْتِ. — The room is in the house.
- فِي الْغُرْفَةِ مَكْتَبٌ. — There is a desk in the room.
- عَلَى الْمَكْتَبِ كِتَابٌ. — There is a book on the desk.
- قُرْبَ الْمَكْتَبِ كُرْسِيٌّ. — There is a chair near the desk.
- فِي الْغُرْفَةِ قَلَمٌ. — There is a pen in the room.
